scope.summary: Implement automatic motion conversion from imported FBX animation tracks to Tailor's canonical AnimationDraft (AvatarBonePoseTrack entries), including frame-rate normalization, joint-name re-mapping via JointMapper, and emission of a TailorAnimationImported event linking the resulting tailor_animations row
acceptance_criteria: POST /api/tailor/animations/import accepts a multipart FBX file and an avt_id query parameter
Joint animation tracks are extracted, joint names are re-mapped via JointMapper using the AVT-'s detected naming convention, and an AnimationDraft is constructed with AvatarBonePoseTrack entries
Frame-rate normalization converts the imported FPS to the canonical Timeline.fps (default 24)
A tailor_animations row is written and TailorAnimationImported (new KernelEventType variant) is emitted with event_family='tailor.animation'
Un-mappable joints emit a JointMappingWarning advisory finding in the import result; they are excluded from the AnimationDraft but do not fail the import
cargo test motion_import::test_fbx_24fps_roundtrip and motion_import::test_joint_remap_on_import both pass
scope.risk_if_missed: MT-284 (skeletal pose import BVH/glTF) covers the data types but not the FBX-specific parsing and joint re-mapping pipeline. FBX is the dominant animation export from Maya and 3ds Max and the primary source for character animation in game-adjacent pipelines. Without auto-conversion, operators must pre-process every animation externally.

risks: Broad MT scope can hide multiple review targets.
Markdown MT lists may disagree with packet-declared active/next MT truth.
Receipt routing may target an old MT if contract identity is omitted.
minimum_controls: Exactly one primary proof target per MT unless the packet records why not.
MT identity must be stable and referenced by receipt correlation.
Generated projection drift must not wake Coder or Validator by itself.
